seed_instruction: Write an article titled "How to overcome public speaking anxiety."
org_constraint: Structure the article in JSON format with the following keys: {"title": String, "introduction": String, "body": Array of Strings, "conclusion": String}.
new_constraint: Structure the article in Markdown format, including at least three level-2 headers.
instruction: Write an article titled "How to overcome public speaking anxiety." Structure the article in JSON format with the following keys: {"title": String, "introduction": String, "body": Array of Strings, "conclusion": String}. Structure the article in Markdown format, including at least three level-2 headers.
response:
```json
{
  "title": "How to overcome public speaking anxiety",
  "introduction": "Public speaking anxiety, often referred to as glossophobia, is a common challenge that affects many individuals. Whether you are presenting at work, giving a speech at a community event, or performing on stage, the fear of being judged can be overwhelming. However, with the right strategies and mindset, you can transform this anxiety into confidence and deliver powerful presentations.",
  "body": [
    "### Understand the Root of Your Fear\nBefore you can overcome public speaking anxiety, it is essential to understand what triggers it. For many, the fear stems from a desire to be perfect or a fear of failure. Recognize that your audience is generally on your side and wants you to succeed. Remind yourself that mistakes are human and rarely as noticeable to others as they feel to you.",
    "### Prepare Thoroughly and Practice\nOne of the most effective ways to reduce anxiety is preparation. Know your material inside and out, but avoid memorizing every word, which can lead to rigid delivery. Instead, focus on understanding the key points and practicing your delivery multiple times. Simulate the actual speaking environment by practicing in front of friends, recording yourself, or speaking in front of a mirror. This builds familiarity and reduces the uncertainty that fuels anxiety.",
    "### Use Breathing Techniques and Visualization\nPhysical symptoms of anxiety, such as rapid heartbeat and shallow breathing, can be managed with controlled breathing exercises. Practice deep, diaphragmatic breathing before and during your speech to calm your nervous system. Additionally, visualize yourself succeeding. Imagine walking onto the stage with confidence, delivering your message clearly, and receiving positive feedback. This mental rehearsal primes your brain for success and reduces the likelihood of panic."
  ],
  "conclusion": "Overcoming public speaking anxiety is a journey that requires patience, practice, and self-compassion. By understanding your fears, preparing diligently, and employing relaxation techniques, you can gradually build the confidence needed to speak effectively. Remember that every great speaker once stood where you are now, and with time, you too will find your voice and inspire others."
}
```
